evidence: `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/services/downloadService.ts` and multiple `MediaGalleryItem/*` modules import `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` from `MediaGallery/types`, making item/service layers depend on a parent view-module type owner.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exposes both eager and lazy runtime access (`initializeSupabase` and `getSupabaseClient`), which allows cross-module usage without explicit bootstrap ordering and hides global lifecycle coupling behind a getter.

Tooltip provider ownership is fragmented: `LightboxShell` provides `TooltipProvider` globally for the lightbox subtree, while `CreativePartnerPane` and `ShotActions` instantiate additional local providers with different timing behavior (`delayDuration`), creating cross-module UI behavior coupling via provider nesting.

Lightbox image/video paths rebuild nearly identical boundary models and panel composition separately (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:811`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:178`) instead of sharing one seam contract.

`useSegmentSlotMode` and `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` require broad option bags with many cross-layer callbacks/state handles (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:26`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:31`), increasing integration friction.

Task payload translation is spread across multiple task modules with repeated ad-hoc field assembly (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:242`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:549`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:140`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts:150`).

validateClipsForJoin can report a positive maxSafeGap even when a clip cannot satisfy replace-mode constraints, because maxSafeGap is clamped to at least 1 before validity is checked (src/tools/join-clips/utils/validation.ts:176-181, :193-197).

Frame-count defaults are inconsistent across adjacent workflows (ASSUMED_FPS=30 vs 24/16 fallback logic), which creates contradictory local behavior for similar calculations (src/tools/training-data-helper/components/VideoSegmentEditor/constants.ts:2, src/tools/join-clips/utils/validation.ts:129-133).

taskConfig mixes cache conversion, fallback mapping, and policy defaults in one path with implicit behavior (always canCancel=true from DB rows), reducing local clarity and increasing hidden behavior coupling (src/shared/lib/taskConfig.ts:33-40, :47-69).

`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts` models a denied limit as `operationSuccess({ allowed: false })` while other shared edge guards (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts`) model invalid requests as `operationFailure(...)`, creating mixed success/failure signaling at the same API layer.

`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` exposes non-throwing status unions (`VariantProjectScopeStatus`), but `src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts` wraps those statuses into thrown exceptions (`LineageScopeError`) for the same domain lookup path, forcing callers to handle different contracts for equivalent scope failures.

`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts` accepts an `AbortSignal` but dedupes by `{toolId,projectId,shotId}` only; when an inflight promise exists, callers receive shared completion regardless of their own signal lifecycle.

Multiple tools/* entrypoints are now compatibility re-export shells with no domain logic (src/tools/image-generation/components/ImageGenerationForm/index.tsx, src/tools/image-generation/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts, src/tools/travel-between-images/components/PhaseConfigVertical.tsx, src/tools/join-clips/settings.ts).

Deprecated aliases still expose full behavior at runtime instead of forcing migration, e.g. handleError/handleAndRethrow and toolConstants proxies (src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts, src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts).

Legacy travel structure fields are duplicated across canonical and legacy surfaces, increasing migration drift risk (src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts, src/shared/types/referenceImage.ts).

`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` mixes `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow(...)` with `throw normalizeAndPresentError(...)`, then applies additional `onError` normalization in mutation handlers.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` normalizes before throwing in inner DB lookup branches, then catches and rethrows through `rethrowTaskCreationError(...)`, creating multi-stage error transformation in one flow.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` rethrows from UI callbacks, while sibling UI hooks (`useMediaGalleryItemState`, `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks`) normalize and return recoverable results instead of throwing.
`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/services/downloadService.ts` throws ad-hoc raw `Error` values at I/O boundaries, unlike neighboring gallery modules that attach normalized context/log metadata.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error handling policy is not consistently enforced at layer boundaries (UI callbacks, task creation orchestration, and download I/O), causing different propagation semantics for similar failures.

findings.summary: Media gallery item and service modules depend on a type owned by a higher-level view module, inverting dependency direction.
evidence: `downloadService.ts` imports `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` from `@/shared/components/MediaGallery/types`.
`InfoTooltip.tsx`, `ShotActions.tsx`, and `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` import the same type from `../../MediaGallery/types`.
This creates a shared dependency from lower-level item/service code back to a parent feature module instead of a neutral domain/shared contract.
findings.suggestion: Move `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` into a neutral shared contract module (for example `src/shared/contracts/media.ts` or a domain type package), then have both `MediaGallery` and `MediaGalleryItem` import from that contract.

findings.summary: High-level runtime facades hide global lifecycle/state behavior, making initialization order implicit across modules.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` is documented as "safe before bootstrap (lazy initializes when needed)", so any importer can trigger runtime initialization without explicit app-lifecycle coordination.
`runtimeError.ts` centralizes reporting/throwing entrypoints and is a major import hub, which similarly funnels side-effectful behavior behind globally reachable helpers.
Both files act as broad cross-module entrypoints where call sites cannot distinguish pure access from stateful runtime actions.
findings.suggestion: Split pure access from side-effectful initialization/reporting by contract: require explicit bootstrap wiring at app composition roots, then inject initialized collaborators (Supabase client, error reporter) into feature modules instead of exposing globally callable lazy entrypoints.

findings.summary: Tooltip provider ownership is distributed across feature components, causing cross-module behavioral coupling and inconsistent interaction policy.
evidence: `LightboxShell` wraps its whole dialog with `TooltipProvider delayDuration={500}`.
`ShotActions` creates another local `TooltipProvider delayDuration={0}` for action buttons.
`CreativePartnerPane` also creates a local `TooltipProvider` around its ecosystem tooltip.

The shared tooltip surface exports primitives only, but provider placement/policy is left to leaf modules, producing nested providers and behavior differences that are hard to reason about system-wide.
findings.suggestion: Define a single tooltip policy boundary (for example app-shell or feature-shell providers) and keep leaf components provider-free; expose only feature-level override points when behavior must differ.

findings.summary: Image/video lightbox seams duplicate orchestration contracts instead of sharing one boundary model.
evidence: Image path builds large `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, and `layoutProps` objects with long dependency arrays (`ImageLightbox.tsx:753-842`).
Video path repeats parallel object construction and panel branching (`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:14-142`, `:178-260`) with mostly equivalent shape names and responsibilities.
`LightboxLayout` consumes broad context slices (`LightboxLayout.tsx:38-77`) and assumes both paths supply compatible structures, so duplication creates drift risk at the seam.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared lightbox render-model factory (common core/media/variant/navigation + panel contract), then keep only media-specific deltas (video edit vs image edit) in thin adapters.

findings.summary: Shot editor hooks rely on oversized option/state bags that blur module boundaries.
evidence: `UseSegmentSlotModeResolvedProps` includes many unrelated concerns (navigation, structure video mutation, frame limits, pending state coordination) in one input interface (`useSegmentSlotMode.ts:26-50`).
`UseShotImagesEditorCallbacksOptions` combines UI callbacks, data model slices, and persistence actions in a single options object (`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:31-40`).
`useInlineEditState` similarly composes broad environment/persistence/edit submodels and returns large state surfaces (`useInlineEditState.ts:63-176`), showing repeated seam pattern.
findings.suggestion: Split these hook contracts into smaller capability objects (navigation capability, structure-video capability, mutation capability), and compose them at call sites to keep each handoff minimal and testable.

findings.summary: Task payload construction is fragmented across modules with inconsistent field-mapping style.
evidence: `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` manually assembles a large orchestrator object with many conditionals (`payloadBuilder.ts:242-301`).
`individualTravelSegment` separately builds overlapping task payload fields (`individualTravelSegment.ts:541-625`, `:627-697`) with another custom optional-field pass.
`magicEdit` and `videoEnhance` implement additional bespoke payload builders (`magicEdit.ts:135-184`, `videoEnhance.ts:147-219`) for similar concerns (defaults, optional metadata, lineage fields).
findings.suggestion: Create a shared task-payload composition layer (base metadata + lineage + optional-field policy + tool-specific extension points) and migrate individual task modules to that single translation pipeline.

findings.summary: DataFreshnessManager internals mix policy logic with UI effects and error semantics.
evidence: `DataFreshnessManager` contains polling policy (`getPollingInterval`), circuit breaker accounting, error classification, and direct `toast.error` emission in one class (`DataFreshnessManager.ts:91-195`, `:200-215`).
`useTasks` directly triggers freshness notifications in query functions (`useTasks.ts:37-45`), tightening coupling between fetch internals and manager side-effect behavior.
findings.suggestion: Extract pure policy modules (polling decision + failure classification) from `DataFreshnessManager`, keep the manager as state coordinator only, and move toast emission to a subscribed UI adapter.

findings.summary: Rate-limit denial is encoded as a successful operation result, diverging from neighboring edge APIs that use failure envelopes for request rejection.
evidence: `checkRateLimit` returns `operationSuccess({ allowed: false, ... })` for exceeded limits (`rateLimit.ts:172-181`), while method violations in `ensureRequestMethod` return `operationFailure` (`edgeRequest.ts:61-67`).
Callers need special branching helpers (`isRateLimitExceededFailure`) to interpret an effective error state carried on an ok result (`rateLimit.ts:275-280`).
findings.suggestion: Normalize edge guard semantics so denied limits are represented as `operationFailure` with a stable `rate_limit_exceeded` code and structured decision data in `cause`; keep `allowed:false` only as response payload shaping, not as the primary control-flow contract.

findings.summary: Generation/variant scope resolution exposes incompatible failure contracts (status unions vs thrown exceptions) across adjacent consumers.
evidence: `resolveVariantProjectScope` returns a typed status union and never throws for expected not-found/scope outcomes (`generationTaskRepository.ts:128-196`).

`fetchVariantById` in `useLineageChain` converts non-`ok` statuses into thrown `LineageScopeError` (`useLineageChain.ts:95-105`) and throws normalized runtime errors for query misses (`useLineageChain.ts:112-118`).
`preloadGenerationTaskMappings` consumes the same mapping/status approach without throw-based flow (`generationTaskCache.ts:52-77`).
findings.suggestion: Choose one boundary contract for scope lookup (prefer explicit result/status object) and adapt all consumers to it; if throwing is required in hooks, centralize conversion in one adapter function so repository semantics remain uniform.

findings.summary: Tool settings fetch deduplication can violate per-caller cancellation semantics by reusing inflight work regardless of caller signal state.
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` dedupe key omits signal and returns shared inflight promise (`toolSettingsService.ts:365-370`).

A caller that aborts after dedupe attaches still awaits shared resolution, unlike timeout/cancellation-first contracts elsewhere (e.g., `runTimelineWriteWithTimeout` aborts and maps timeout to typed error in `timelineWriteQueue.ts:54-83`).
findings.suggestion: Wrap shared inflight promises with per-caller cancellation gates (race shared promise with signal abort) so dedupe preserves each caller’s cancellation contract.

findings.summary: Travel-between-images boundary types blend UI, migration, and API transport concerns, pushing heavy translation logic into a single orchestration service.
evidence: `types.ts` contains UI config (`PromptConfig`, `MotionConfig`, `ModelConfig`), transport DTOs (`TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`), migration notes, and stitch orchestration config together.

`generateVideoService.ts` performs broad multi-step assembly (`buildImagePayload`, pair overrides, model selection, request-body shaping) and exports many internals for tests (`generateVideoService.ts:539-560`), indicating a high-friction seam.
findings.suggestion: Split contracts into distinct layers: `ui-state`, `domain-intent`, and `api-dto`; move request-body assembly into a dedicated mapper module with a narrow public API consumed by `generateVideo`.

findings.summary: Join-clips form contracts conflate mutable controller callbacks, feature flags, and view concerns, making integration seams brittle and difficult to evolve.
evidence: `JoinClipsClipSettings` and `JoinClipsMotionConfig` mix value props with many setter callbacks and optional toggles (`types.ts:20-93`), then flatten all groups via spread (`types.ts:106-112`).
`clipInitService` still carries explicit new-vs-legacy settings branching (`clipInitService.ts:322-389`), indicating boundary drift not absorbed by a stable adapter.
`metadata.ts` returns `OperationResult` while the form contracts are callback-driven mutable interfaces, reinforcing multiple seam styles within the same feature.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single join-clips view-model contract (immutable state + command handlers) and a dedicated legacy-settings adapter at the boundary, then remove flattened spread-based prop resolution.

findings.summary: Compatibility re-export shells are proliferating and preserving duplicate import paths long after moves, creating migration drag and AI-style boilerplate debt.
evidence: Each file is mostly comment + re-export, with no functional logic.
All four are explicitly marked as backwards-compatibility adapters, indicating the migration has left dual public paths active.
Pattern repetition suggests a structural migration strategy issue rather than isolated local necessity.
findings.suggestion: Set explicit removal targets per shim, add lint/import restrictions to block new imports from legacy tool paths, and collapse call sites to canonical shared paths in one coordinated migration sweep.

findings.summary: Deprecated alias modules still execute full production behavior, so deprecation warnings do not meaningfully reduce future migration debt.
evidence: handleError.ts marks APIs deprecated with a removal target but still forwards directly to runtimeError behavior.
toolConstants.ts wraps canonical objects with Proxy and keeps old exports callable, preserving old API indefinitely unless imports are actively cleaned.
Both modules rely on runtime signaling instead of compile-time enforcement, which allows debt growth.
findings.suggestion: Move these aliases to strict bridge mode: type-level deprecation + CI guard on new imports now, then throw in non-production (or fail tests) after grace period, and finally remove the alias exports on the stated target date.

findings.summary: Join-clips validation computes 'safe' slider maxima that can be non-zero even when replace-mode settings are impossible for the shortest clip.
evidence: In replace mode, maxSafeGap is clamped with Math.max(1, shortestClipFrames - 2 * contextFrameCount), so impossible states still report at least 1 safe gap.
Function later marks config invalid via required.total checks, so returned 'safe' values and validity can contradict each other.
This creates confusing local control flow for callers expecting returned limits to reflect valid space.
findings.suggestion: When shortest clip cannot satisfy minimum constraints, return 0-safe bounds (or explicit impossible-state metadata) before quantization, and keep safe-limit outputs consistent with the validity check.

findings.summary: Adjacent media flows use conflicting implicit FPS defaults, causing inconsistent frame estimations for similar user inputs.
evidence: Training-data helper assumes 30 FPS globally.
Join-clips fallback path assumes 24 FPS when using input FPS and 16 FPS otherwise.
No shared policy source is used, so low-level calculations diverge by module rather than by explicit product rule.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared FPS policy helper/constants module with named scenarios (source-native, backend-default, estimation-default), then update both modules to consume it explicitly.

findings.summary: Canonical facade boundaries are declared in docs but not technically enforced in code.
evidence: README.md marks these files as canonical contract surfaces and ties them to governance gates.
runtimeError.ts and client.ts only forward to deeper modules and do not include any boundary guard (e.g., path-level import restrictions or package-level encapsulation).
This allows consumers to bypass canonical facades by importing implementation modules directly, undermining the documented architecture contract.
findings.suggestion: Add enforceable import-boundary rules (for example eslint no-restricted-imports or boundary plugin rules) that require app code to import via canonical facades for error handling and Supabase runtime access, and wire those checks into the listed governance gates.

findings.summary: Contract entrypoints use inconsistent contract markers, reducing governance rule reliability.
evidence: runtimeError.ts and client.ts use `@publicContract`.
contracts/cn.ts uses `@uiContract` instead of the same marker family.
button.tsx and generation/types/public.ts expose canonical surfaces but rely on plain comments without a shared structured contract tag.
findings.suggestion: Standardize a single machine-parseable contract annotation schema across all canonical entrypoints (or replace comment tags with a central manifest) and update governance checks to validate that every declared surface is tagged consistently.

findings.summary: Compatibility lifecycle policy is tracked in README table but not co-located with exported contract APIs.
evidence: README.md includes a removal target date (2026-06-30) for deprecated error-handling aliases.
runtimeError.ts exports canonical APIs but does not carry lifecycle/version metadata tied to compatibility policy in the surface itself.
findings.suggestion: Co-locate compatibility metadata with the contract surface (e.g., explicit deprecated export wrappers with removal version/date metadata and test assertions) so lifecycle policy can be validated directly from code instead of drifting in documentation tables.

findings.summary: Some flows normalize/present an error and then rethrow into another normalizer, causing duplicate transformation and inconsistent telemetry context.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` throws `normalizeAndPresentError(variantError, ...)` (line ~250), but mutation-level `onError` handlers also call `normalizeAndPresentError(...)`, so one failure can be normalized multiple times.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` throws `normalizeAndPresentError(...)` during child lookup failures (lines ~810, ~834), then outer `catch` calls `rethrowTaskCreationError(...)` (line ~880), layering another error-normalization path on top.
findings.suggestion: Apply one normalization boundary per flow: either throw raw domain errors and normalize once at the outer boundary, or use `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow(...)` as the only throwing path and remove secondary normalization in callers.

findings.summary: UI event handlers use conflicting failure semantics: some rethrow after normalization while sibling handlers convert failures to recoverable UI-safe results.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` catches and calls `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow(...)` in user-triggered navigation callbacks (`handleNavigateToShot`, `handleVisitShotFromNotifier`).

`useMediaGalleryItemState` and `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` catch similar UI action failures and return/continue with `operationFailure(...)` or local fallback behavior after `normalizeAndPresentError(...)`, without rethrowing.
findings.suggestion: Define a UI-layer policy (non-throwing command handlers returning result objects) and reserve throwing for non-UI orchestration layers; convert `useMediaGalleryHandlers` to match the non-throwing contract.

findings.summary: Download service emits raw ad-hoc errors at network boundaries instead of the normalized error contract used by adjacent gallery logic.
evidence: `downloadService.ts` rejects/throws raw `Error` strings from XHR/fetch branches (`Network request failed`, `Download request was aborted`, `Failed to fetch image: ...`) with no shared `context`/`logData` envelope.
Adjacent gallery hooks route failures through `normalizeAndPresentError(...)` with explicit context and toast policy, so download-path failures follow a separate, less-instrumented error contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared download error wrapper (or `OperationResult` return type) that preserves status/url/context and normalize at one boundary before UI presentation.
